adds.rationale: Adds a diversified robotics/automation ETF sleeve to complement single-name BOTZ, giving broader exposure to autonomy and industrial automation as venture and capex flow into the space.

news_evidence.summary: Tesla broadens Robotaxi commercial service footprint, signaling autonomy commercialization moving past pilot phase.
Q2-25 hyperscaler capex prints reinforce that AI infrastructure spend is spilling into adjacent automation and robotics suppliers.

adds.rationale: Adds the sole-source advanced-node foundry that underpins the entire AI accelerator stack; Nvidia's FY26 capex guidance implies a multi-year pull on TSMC's leading-edge capacity.

news_evidence.summary: Nvidia FY26 capex/spend guidance points to sustained demand for leading-edge wafer capacity through 2026.
Hyperscaler Q2-25 capex commentary reiterates accelerator supply remains the binding constraint, with TSMC named as the bottleneck.

removes.rationale: Trim quantum exposure to zero (no current watchlist coverage); commercial milestones remain thin relative to robotics and AI-infrastructure adds being made this quarter.

news_evidence.summary: Quantum hardware names remain pre-revenue with milestones still framed as research benchmarks rather than commercial inflections through 2025.

removes.rationale: Replaced by ARKQ which offers broader autonomy/robotics exposure; keeps the robotics sleeve at one ETF rather than stacking two overlapping thematic funds.

news_evidence.summary: Robotaxi commercial expansion and adjacent automation spend favor a broader autonomy basket over BOTZ's narrower industrial-robotics tilt.

rationale_overall: Net watchlist size unchanged at 12. Swap deepens AI-infrastructure exposure (TSM) at the foundry chokepoint, and broadens the robotics sleeve (ARKQ for BOTZ). Quantum trimmed to zero to avoid stacking pre-revenue names while the nuclear and AI buckets carry stronger near-term commercial signals.
